title: Browser-surface smoke coverage must follow the actual clicked user path, auth re
rationale: This is a reusable web-testing pattern. Build and route checks alone did not catch a user-visible auth/navigation failure; browser-surface smoke assertions materially improve reliability across web projects.
description: Browser-surface smoke coverage must follow the actual clicked user path, auth redirects, and session state, not just build success or route availability. A newly added Login/Dashboard CTA passed build-level checks but failed immediately in real use because the redirect target and auth flow were wrong. Smoke coverage should assert the rendered CTA target, follow redirects, tolerate stale-session cookies, and exercise the sign-in path that a user actually clicks.
decision_reason: Category: other. Impact: 8/10. Defer until higher-priority signals processed.
